question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted stream file that causes the parser to stall beyond the processingTimeout (default 10s), triggering the health indicator to report DOWN and causing the importer node to be restarted or removed from service, affecting >=30% of mirror nodes if deployed uniformly?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the stream source with a high volume of valid-but-large stream files such that the parser loop driven by getFrequency() (default 20ms) cannot drain the queue fast enough, causing sustained processing lag that trips the processingTimeout health check and shuts down >=30% of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file containing exactly BatchProperties.maxItems (default 60,000) records each with maximum-size payloads, causing the batch flush to exhaust JVM heap memory and crash >=30% of importer processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit stream files that saturate the BatchProperties.queueCapacity (default 10) blocking queue, causing the BatchStreamFileNotifier subscriber thread to block indefinitely on queue.put(), starving the parser and causing the processingTimeout health check to fail on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfigured actuator endpoint or environment variable injection to call setEnabled(false) on the ParserProperties bean, disabling stream processing on >=30% of importer nodes without requiring authentication?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit an exposed Spring Boot Actuator /env or /configprops endpoint (if misconfigured without authentication) to set hiero.mirror.importer.parser.record.enabled=false via a POST request, effectively disabling the record parser on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malformed record stream file that causes a deadlock inside the parser's transaction handling, holding the processing thread past the processingTimeout window and causing the AbstractStreamFileHealthIndicator to mark the node unhealthy, triggering a pod restart on >=30% of Kubernetes-deployed importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a single record item whose deserialization triggers unbounded recursion or deep object graph traversal, causing a StackOverflowError that crashes the parser thread and halts processing on >=30% of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file where BatchProperties.maxFiles (default 1) is respected but the single file contains a deeply nested protobuf structure that causes the parser to spend more time than processingTimeout allows, causing health check failure and node shutdown on >=30% of importer instances?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user publish a stream file with a mismatched or corrupted StreamType header that causes the parser to enter an unhandled exception path, bypassing retry logic and permanently halting the processing loop on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that processingTimeout defaults to only 10 seconds by submitting a stream file that triggers a slow database query (e.g., via a crafted contract state change with maximum slot data), causing the DB transaction to exceed processingTimeout and the health check to mark >=30% of nodes as DOWN?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes the BatchProperties.flushInterval (default 2s) timer to fire repeatedly without completing a flush due to a persistent DB error, causing the parser to accumulate unbounded in-memory state and eventually trigger an OutOfMemoryError on >=30% of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes the BatchProperties.window (default 5 minutes) to be exceeded without completing, holding a database transaction open for the full window duration and causing connection pool exhaustion that shuts down >=30% of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 20ms polling frequency of getFrequency() by publishing a continuous stream of minimally-valid but computationally expensive stream files, causing CPU saturation that degrades the JVM to the point where health check threads are starved and >=30% of nodes are marked unhealthy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with exactly BatchProperties.maxItems records that each trigger a separate database upsert, causing the batch persister to hold a single database transaction open long enough to exceed the transactionTimeout (default 120s) and crash the parser on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Cloud Config or Kubernetes ConfigMap update race condition to toggle isEnabled() to false during active parsing, causing the SqlEntityListener.onEnd() to skip flushing and leaving the database in an inconsistent state that causes subsequent parser startup failures on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that triggers a GC pause (via allocation of large byte arrays during parsing) lasting longer than processingTimeout (10s), causing the health indicator to incorrectly report the node as DOWN and triggering an automated restart on >=30% of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes the queueCapacity (default 10) ArrayBlockingQueue in BatchStreamFileNotifier to fill up, causing the notify() method's queue.put() call to block the downloader thread indefinitely, halting both downloading and parsing on >=30% of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user publish a stream file with a valid StreamType but corrupted internal structure that causes the parser to throw an uncaught RuntimeException, bypassing the retry mechanism and permanently stopping the StreamFileSubscriber.run() loop on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file containing a record with a contract state change whose slot byte array is at maximum protobuf size, causing the BatchUpserter to generate a SQL statement that exceeds the database's max_allowed_packet or statement length limit, causing a fatal DB error that crashes the parser on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the tight processingTimeout (default 10s) by submitting a stream file that triggers a Redis publish operation (via RedisPublisher) that blocks on a slow Redis connection, causing the total processing time to exceed processingTimeout and the health check to fail on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that causes the BatchProperties.maxItems (60,000) limit to be reached with records that each allocate large byte arrays (e.g., transaction bytes), causing the JVM to exceed its -Xmx heap limit and trigger an OutOfMemoryError that kills the importer process on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 20ms getFrequency() polling interval by publishing a stream of files that each cause a brief but non-zero processing delay, creating a cumulative lag that causes the processingTimeout health check to fire and mark >=30% of importer nodes as unhealthy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file that causes the BatchProperties.window (5 minutes) to be consumed by a single large batch, preventing any other stream files from being processed during that window and causing the processingTimeout health check to expire on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit an unauthenticated Spring Boot Actuator /refresh endpoint (in a Spring Cloud Config setup) to reload configuration that sets isEnabled() to false, disabling the parser on >=30% of importer nodes without direct code access?
